fields.summary: [SECURITY] Fix JWT alg:none validation bypass in auth middleware

content.text: Critical: auth middleware accepts tokens with alg:none header, bypassing signature verification. Any attacker can forge arbitrary tokens. Fix: whitelist only HS256/RS256 at parse time. Linked to Sentry AUTH-001 and CVE-2026-1337. PR #840 open on hotfix/auth-regression branch.

issuetype.description: A software defect causing incorrect behavior.
issuetype.name: Bug

content.text: Found this during postmortem. Linked to AUTH-001 in Sentry — 2847 occurrences in the last 12 hours. PR #840 is ready for review.
Reviewing now. The whitelist approach is correct — rejecting at parse time is safer than checking after the fact. Will approve once tests pass.
Added a fuzz test that generates tokens with various alg headers. All non-whitelisted algorithms correctly rejected.

customfield_10020.name: Sprint 42 — Security & Stability
customfield_10020.goal: Resolve auth regression, merge hotfix PRs, and close out v3.0 release blockers.

fields.summary: [SECURITY] Enforce strict audience claim validation in JWT verify

content.text: Token verification does not check the aud claim. Tokens issued for other services (email-service, notification-service) are accepted by the API. Fix: require aud == 'api.acme.example.com' — reject all others. Linked to Sentry AUTH-002.

issuetype.description: A software defect causing incorrect behavior.
issuetype.name: Bug

content.text: 1203 occurrences in Sentry (AUTH-002). This is exploitable — any service in our ecosystem with a JWT can impersonate users on the API.
PR #841 up. Tests cover: correct audience passes, wrong audience fails, missing audience fails, multiple audiences (only exact match passes).

customfield_10020.name: Sprint 42 — Security & Stability
customfield_10020.goal: Resolve auth regression, merge hotfix PRs, and close out v3.0 release blockers.

content.text: The /auth/token endpoint has no rate limiting, enabling credential stuffing at high volume. Add IP-based sliding window rate limit: 20 req/min with exponential backoff penalty, 15-minute account lockout after 10 consecutive failures. PR #842.
Using Redis sorted sets for the sliding window counter. The penalty backoff doubles the lockout window for each subsequent lockout.
Make sure X-Forwarded-For is trusted only from known load balancers — otherwise attackers can spoof the IP header to bypass rate limiting.

customfield_10020.name: Sprint 42 — Security & Stability
customfield_10020.goal: Resolve auth regression, merge hotfix PRs, and close out v3.0 release blockers.

fields.summary: Fix token refresh race condition causing duplicate refresh calls

content.text: Concurrent requests from the same user session can trigger multiple simultaneous token refresh API calls. Each returns a new token, but all except the first are then immediately invalidated. Causes intermittent 401s. Fix: singleflight on refresh by session ID. PR #821 merged.

issuetype.description: A software defect causing incorrect behavior.
issuetype.name: Bug

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

content.text: PR #821 merged. The singleflight approach worked cleanly — tested with 50 concurrent goroutines hitting refresh simultaneously, only 1 upstream call made.

customfield_10020.name: Sprint 42 — Security & Stability
customfield_10020.goal: Resolve auth regression, merge hotfix PRs, and close out v3.0 release blockers.

fields.summary: Session timeout not propagating to WebSocket connections

content.text: When a user session expires, the HTTP API correctly rejects requests with 401, but existing WebSocket connections for that user remain open indefinitely. The WS handler does not listen for session invalidation events. Fix: broadcast channel for session expiry; WS handler closes on signal. PR #832.

issuetype.description: A software defect causing incorrect behavior.
issuetype.name: Bug

content.text: Reproduced: open WS connection, wait for session TTL (30min in staging), observe connection remains open. Can send/receive messages 10+ minutes after session expiry.
PR #832 ready for review. Used a broadcast channel with a context-derived cancellation — the WS goroutine selects on it and sends close frame.
LGTM on the approach. Left one comment: we should also handle the case where the WS connection outlives the server restart.

customfield_10020.name: Sprint 42 — Security & Stability
customfield_10020.goal: Resolve auth regression, merge hotfix PRs, and close out v3.0 release blockers.

fields.summary: Add OAuth2 PKCE flow for third-party integrations

content.text: Implement OAuth2 PKCE (Proof Key for Code Exchange) for public clients integrating with our API. Removes need for client secrets. New endpoints: POST /oauth/authorize, POST /oauth/token. State parameter validated server-side. PR #820.
RFC 7636 compliant implementation. Using S256 challenge method only (plain is insecure). Authorization codes are single-use, 10-minute TTL.
One concern: are we storing the code_verifier server-side or doing it stateless? Stateless is better for scalability.
Stateless — the challenge is stored in the authorization code (encrypted). Verifier is provided at token exchange time. No server-side state needed.

customfield_10020.name: Sprint 42 — Security & Stability
customfield_10020.goal: Resolve auth regression, merge hotfix PRs, and close out v3.0 release blockers.

fields.summary: Rate limiting per API key using Redis sliding window

content.text: Implement per-API-key rate limiting with configurable tiers. Use Redis sorted sets for sliding window counter. Expose X-RateLimit-* headers. Tiers: free (100/min), pro (1000/min), enterprise (configurable). PR #828.
Redis ZADD with NX flag gives us atomic sliding window. ZRANGEBYSCORE + ZCARD gives us the count. All in a single MULTI/EXEC transaction.
Make sure you're using PEXPIRE with millisecond precision — second-precision TTLs can cause up to 1000ms of burst over the limit.

customfield_10020.name: Sprint 42 — Security & Stability
customfield_10020.goal: Resolve auth regression, merge hotfix PRs, and close out v3.0 release blockers.

fields.summary: Dashboard N+1 query fix: batch fetch widget data

content.text: Dashboard loads widget data with one SQL query per widget (up to 24). Replacing with single batch query using ANY($1::uuid[]) parameter. P99 load time reduced from 4.2s to 340ms in staging. PR #822.
Benchmark results: 24-widget dashboard, P50 1.1s → 210ms, P99 4.2s → 340ms. The batch query is 8 lines vs 24 separate queries.
LGTM. One nit: the lookup map should use a sync.Map if it could be accessed concurrently, but given the render is single-goroutine, a plain map is fine.

customfield_10020.name: Sprint 42 — Security & Stability
customfield_10020.goal: Resolve auth regression, merge hotfix PRs, and close out v3.0 release blockers.

fields.summary: User activity audit log: structured append-only event store

content.text: Add immutable audit log for user actions: login, logout, password change, API key lifecycle, permission changes. Stored in append-only audit_events table. Queryable via /admin/audit endpoint with filtering by user, action type, date range. Draft PR #831.
Schema draft: audit_events(id uuid, user_id uuid, action text, resource_type text, resource_id text, metadata jsonb, created_at timestamptz). Index on (user_id, created_at DESC).
Make sure the table is append-only at the DB level — no UPDATE or DELETE permissions for the app user. Only a dedicated audit-admin role can purge (with retention policy enforcement).
Compliance note: we need to retain audit logs for 7 years (SOC2 requirement). Make sure the retention policy respects this.

customfield_10020.name: Sprint 42 — Security & Stability
customfield_10020.goal: Resolve auth regression, merge hotfix PRs, and close out v3.0 release blockers.

fields.summary: Revoke all sessions issued with vulnerable JWT library

content.text: Sessions issued between 2026-02-01 and 2026-03-14 (when the vulnerable jwt library was in use) must be force-expired. Need a migration to mark those sessions as revoked and a background job to clean up cached tokens. PR #843.
Scope: approximately 1.2M sessions need to be invalidated. We should do this in batches to avoid DB lock contention. Batch size: 10k, delay: 100ms between batches.

customfield_10020.name: Sprint 42 — Security & Stability
customfield_10020.goal: Resolve auth regression, merge hotfix PRs, and close out v3.0 release blockers.

content.text: go-jwt v4.5.0 has CVE-2026-1337 (algorithm confusion attack). Upgrade to v5.2.1. Breaking API changes in v5 require updating all call sites. Fixes the underlying library issue that required PRs #840 and #841 as band-aids. PR #844.
v5 API changes: jwt.Parse now returns (*jwt.Token, error) instead of (*jwt.Token, error, []error). MapClaims replaced by StructuredClaims interface. Should be 1-2 day migration.

customfield_10020.name: Sprint 42 — Security & Stability
customfield_10020.goal: Resolve auth regression, merge hotfix PRs, and close out v3.0 release blockers.

fields.summary: Add database connection pool configuration (max_open_conns, idle TTL)

content.text: Current SQL pool uses Go defaults: unlimited connections, no idle TTL. In load tests, we see unbounded growth under sustained traffic. Add config knobs: max_open_conns (default 100), max_idle_conns (default 20), conn_max_lifetime (default 30m), conn_max_idle_time (default 5m). PR #833.
Load test data: at 200 rps, pool climbs to 340 connections after 10 minutes. PG max_connections is 400 — we're leaving almost nothing for admin connections and read replicas.

customfield_10020.name: Sprint 42 — Security & Stability
customfield_10020.goal: Resolve auth regression, merge hotfix PRs, and close out v3.0 release blockers.

fields.summary: Fix CORS preflight failing for OPTIONS requests

content.text: OPTIONS requests were hitting the auth middleware before the CORS handler, returning 401 instead of 200 with CORS headers. Reordered middleware chain to run CORS first. Fixed analytics dashboard fetch errors (Sentry FE-002). PR #830 merged.

issuetype.description: A software defect causing incorrect behavior.
issuetype.name: Bug

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

content.text: One-line fix: move `cors.Handler()` before `auth.RequireToken()` in the middleware stack. Verified with curl: OPTIONS now returns 200 with correct headers.
Sentry FE-002 (NetworkError on /api/v1/analytics/summary) should resolve once this deploys. 447 users affected.

customfield_10020.name: Sprint 42 — Security & Stability
customfield_10020.goal: Resolve auth regression, merge hotfix PRs, and close out v3.0 release blockers.

fields.summary: Fix pagination cursor off-by-one in list endpoints

content.text: List endpoints returned offset+limit+1 as next cursor, causing last item of each page to be duplicated in next page. Fixed cursor to offset+len(results). Regression test added with page boundary assertions. PR #825 merged.

issuetype.description: A software defect causing incorrect behavior.
issuetype.name: Bug

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

content.text: Found via the mobile app team — they saw duplicate items when paginating through search results. Affected all endpoints using the shared paginate() helper.

customfield_10020.name: Sprint 42 — Security & Stability
customfield_10020.goal: Resolve auth regression, merge hotfix PRs, and close out v3.0 release blockers.

fields.summary: Fix memory leak in WebSocket connection pool

content.text: WebSocket connections were not removed from the pool map on abnormal closure (code 1006). Over time, leaked goroutines and memory — production servers needed weekly restart. Fixed by registering cleanup in OnClose regardless of close code. PR #827 merged.

issuetype.description: A software defect causing incorrect behavior.
issuetype.name: Bug

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

content.text: Production goroutine count was growing at ~12/hour. After 1 week: ~2000 leaked goroutines, ~340MB leaked heap. With the fix, goroutine count is stable.
Root cause: OnClose was only called for normal closures (code 1000). Code 1006 (abnormal, network-level) bypassed our cleanup. Fixed by deferring pool removal in the connection goroutine.
Verified fix with a 24-hour soak test: goroutine count stays flat at 142 (expected) even with 500 concurrent connections that are force-dropped every 10 minutes.
